concerns.text: Task T017 implements KNN imputation using 'stoichiometry similarity (normalized element counts)' and Euclidean distance, while the plan.md (Phase 1, Task 1.4) and research.md explicitly mandate 'structure-based KNN (Morgan fingerprints)'. This is a semantic weakening of the data engineering approach defined in the plan, violating the constraint that tasks must not silently relax the plan's methodological requirements.

Task T014 implements alignment using only 'composition' and 'surface_facet' and explicitly excludes 'synthesis_condition'. While the plan pivots to OC20-only (which lacks synthesis_condition), the spec FR-002 mandates alignment on 'composition, surface_facet, synthesis_condition'. The task silently drops the FR-002 requirement without a formal spec amendment or explicit 'staged' marker in the task description, constituting a silent constitution drift from the spec's defined alignment logic.

Tasks T028a/b implement Shapiro-Wilk testing on the 'distribution of paired differences' (XGBoost error - Baseline error). Spec FR-005 mandates Shapiro-Wilk testing on the 'distribution of absolute errors' to determine normality before choosing the test. This changes the statistical null hypothesis and violates the specific verification logic required by the spec.

Task T012 explicitly implements logic to 'exclude' and 'log the exclusion' of Materials Project and 2025 CO2 datasets. Spec FR-001 mandates the system 'MUST download' these datasets. While the plan justifies this pivot, the task implements a direct violation of the spec's functional requirement without a corresponding 'staged' or 'amended' marker in the task text, creating a silent contradiction between the task deliverable and the spec.

Tasks T037/T038 treat SC-003 verification as a binary 'pass/fail' status in JSON. Spec SC-003 requires measuring the R² of the reduced model against the full model to 'measure feature redundancy'. The task focuses on the boolean outcome rather than the quantitative measurement and reporting of the ratio, potentially weakening the evidence required by the success criterion.
